title: Add create_schematic operation
description: The kicad-agent has no operation to create a new .kicad_sch file. All 19+ operations require an existing target file. Add a create_schematic op that generates a valid empty KiCad schematic with proper header, UUID, paper size, and title block. Also consider create_pcb and create_project variants. Schema is in src/kicad_agent/ops/schema.py, executor in src/kicad_agent/ops/executor.py.

title: Add create_symbol operation for new component definitions
description: The kicad-agent has no operation to create new component symbols in .kicad_sym library files. AddLibEntryOp only adds library references to sym-lib-table. Need a create_symbol op that defines a symbol with name, pins (number, name, type, position), graphic elements, and properties (reference, value, footprint, datasheet). This is needed to add new ICs like the AK4619VN codec that don't exist in standard or project libraries.

title: Contextual Placement Constraints
description:
PCB schematics capture electrical connectivity but NOT physical placement intent. Experienced designers carry implicit knowledge ('regulator can\'t be near analog', 'connector must be on edge', 'decoupling cap within 5mm'). kicad-agent has ZERO infrastructure for contextual placement rules.

MISSING:
- Per-component edge/region affinity (J1 must be on board edge)
- Relational proximity (U3 must avoid U1 within 25mm — EMI)
- Relational approach (C4 within 5mm of U3 — decoupling)
- Orientation/facing (LED1 faces up — mechanical)
- Named-region assignment (analog section is left half)

PROPOSED: PlacementRule constraint model
- rule_type: edge_affinity | avoid | approach | orientation | region
- Sources: explicit, inferred, learned, imported
- Enforcement: SA objective, placement gate, routing keepouts
- Persistence: constraints.json sidecar, each rule carries rationale for AI

Related: SchGen (Microsoft 2026), PCBSchemaGen (2026), Phase 104 BlockerDiagnostician, SKIDL integration

title: Phase 108 D-4: Arduino_Mega .kicad_pro file corruption (double extension)
description: tests/fixtures/Arduino_Mega/ has Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro.kicad_pro (double extension — git-state artifact) instead of Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro. KiCad 10 requires the project file to load the schematic; kicad-cli sch export pdf returns 'Failed to load schematic' for BOTH original and autolaid output. Pre-existing fixture infrastructure issue discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 visual verification. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET per four-state taxonomy. Fix: rename file. Readiness signal: this Bead exists; Phase 108 Task 2 on-page guarantee verified via 2 small-fixture PDFs + geometry gate.
notes: PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION: Restored correct Arduino_Mega.kicad_pro filename (was double-extension). Removed stale lock files. HOWEVER kicad-cli still cannot load schematic due to 129 malformed R? symbols — that deeper corruption split out as separate Bead. The .kicad_pro rename portion is DONE; symbol corruption tracked separately.

title: Phase 108 D-5: _parse_symbol_refs no-rotation regex bug
description: src/kicad_agent/schematic_routing/schematic_graph.py:594 _parse_symbol_refs regex requires (at X Y R) — three-number rotation. Symbols without explicit rotation (like Arduino_Mega J1 at (at 100.0 200.0)) are invisible to the topology builder. Pre-existing bug discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 — the on-page parking logic works around it by scanning raw content directly for (symbol (lib_id ...)) blocks, but the underlying parser bug should be fixed. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Fix: change regex to make rotation optional: r'\(symbol\s+\(lib_id\s+"([^"]+)"\)\s+\(at\s+[\d.]+\s+[\d.]+(?:\s+[\d.-]+)?\)'. Affects: any code consuming SchematicGraph.ref_to_libid for symbols without rotation.

title: Phase 108 D-6: SugiyamaLayout Y-axis inversion documented but not implemented
description: src/kicad_agent/schematic_autolayout/sugiyama.py line 11 docstring claims 'Schematic Y is INVERTED (handled in stage 5 coordinate assignment)' but assign_coordinates does NOT implement any inversion — Y grows positive-downward with layer index (y_raw = layer * layer_spacing). Pre-existing gap discovered during Phase 108 Task 2 source review. Doesnt affect on-page guarantee (the layout still fits the page) but the docstring is inaccurate and the visual orientation may not match KiCad eeschema conventions for some boards. Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Fix: either implement the inversion (subtract from page_height) or correct the docstring. Investigate whether KiCad coordinate system actually requires inversion for schematic layout (it does for PCB plot but schematic Y may be non-inverted).

title: Arduino_Mega fixture: 129 malformed R? symbols (missing instances, empty Value)
description: Discovered while fixing D-4 (kicad_pro rename). Even after restoring the correct .kicad_pro filename, kicad-cli sch export pdf returns 'Failed to load schematic' for Arduino_Mega. Root cause: 129 of 143 placed symbols are malformed R? resistors: (1) missing (instances ...) blocks (KiCad 10 requires instances on every placed symbol), (2) empty (property "Value" "") fields, (3) all stacked at (at 50 30). The corruption grew across commits: 6 R? at 5c11d905, 84 at d4591b42, 129 now. The autolayout on-page fix (Phase 108 Task 2) correctly relocates these via UUID-based parking so the SCHEMATIC GEOMETRY is sound (off_page=0, max_stack=1), but KiCad 10.0.3 still refuses to LOAD the file. Fix requires: regenerate Arduino_Mega.kicad_sch from the original Arduino source, OR programmatically repair the 129 malformed symbols (add instances blocks, populate Value, distribute positions). Tracked as DEFERRED-TO-NAMED-TARGET. Priority P2 — blocks visual PDF verification of the medium-fixture class.

title: Document all operations in prompt.md - 12+ missing
description: prompt.md only documents 19 operations but the schema has 31+. Missing: AddWireOp, AddLabelOp, AddPowerOp, AddJunctionOp, AddNoConnectOp, AddNetClassOp, AssignNetClassOp, AddCopperZoneOp, AddDesignRuleOp, AutoRouteOp, RepairSchematicOp, SetBoardOutlineOp, ValidatePowerNetsOp. These all work in the schema but are not documented for LLM consumption.

title: Add component search and symbol import MCP server
description: kicad-agent needs a way to import component symbols from external sources (SnapMagic, Ultra Librarian, LCSC, Octopart). Currently there's no create_symbol operation and no way to fetch existing symbols from part databases. Build an MCP server that: (1) searches for parts by name/MPN, (2) downloads KiCad symbol + footprint into project library, (3) returns pinout/spec data. This would eliminate the manual symbol creation workaround and speed up schematic design across all 22+ modules. Priority sources: SnapMagic (free KiCad symbols), LCSC (already sourcing from), Octopart (aggregator API).

title: Manually created AK4619VN symbol - workaround for missing create_symbol op
description: Workaround: manually created AK4619VN KiCad symbol in shared library because kicad-agent has no create_symbol operation (kicad-agent-2) and no component import MCP (kicad-agent-4). Also manually created pair-card.kicad_sch because no create_schematic operation (kicad-agent-1). Future: automate via create_symbol op + SnapMagic/LCSC MCP.
